input.title: Manufacturing Quality and Inspection Management System
input.description: Manufacturing Quality and Inspection Management System is a web-based enterprise platform that centralizes operational workflows, data visibility, and cross-functional reporting.

user_classes.Admin: Configures the system, manages users, and oversees permissions and settings.
user_classes.Manager: Oversees departmental workflows, approvals, KPIs, and operational reporting.
user_classes.Employee: Performs day-to-day operational tasks within assigned modules and permissions.
user_classes.User: Generic authenticated platform user with access limited by role and department.

definitions.Admin: System user with full administrative privileges.
definitions.Manager: Operational owner of a department, module, or workflow.
definitions.Employee: End user performing role-specific business operations.

scope: Plant and Production Line Setup workflows
constraints: Initial release will focus on the web platform only
Role-based access control is mandatory across all modules
Operational data must be auditable and retained according to policy

operating_environment.frontend: Web-based interface built with React and accessible through modern browsers.
operating_environment.backend: Service layer implemented with Node.js, PostgreSQL, TimescaleDB for business operations.
operating_environment.deployment: Cloud-hosted environment with centralized monitoring, backups, and automated deployments.

NFR-01.title: Security and Access Control
requirements: All sensitive data must be encrypted in transit and at rest
Role-based authorization must restrict access by module and action
All authentication events and privileged actions must be logged

NFR-02.title: Performance and Scalability
requirements: Core pages and dashboards should load within acceptable enterprise SLAs
The platform must support concurrent usage across multiple departments
Background jobs and reporting workloads should not block transactional workflows

NFR-03.title: Reliability and Recoverability
requirements: Automated backups must run on a defined schedule
Critical workflows should support retry-safe processing and audit logging
Operational alerts should be generated for failures and degraded services

system_attributes.reliability: Platform should provide resilient transactional processing with backup and recovery capabilities.
system_attributes.scalability: Architecture should support horizontal scaling for APIs, background workers, and reporting services.
system_attributes.security: Authentication, authorization, encryption, and auditability must be built into every module.
system_attributes.maintainability: Codebase should remain modular, observable, and safe to extend through clear service boundaries.

out_of_scope: Native mobile applications in the first release
Large third-party marketplace or legacy ERP migrations unless explicitly scoped
Non-core experimental modules outside the platform's operational workflows

FR-01.title: Plant and Production Line Setup
requirements: The system must support plant and production line setup as a complete business workflow within the application.
Plant and Production Line Setup should validate required inputs, enforce role-based permissions, and prevent invalid state changes.
Users must be able to review the current state and recent activity associated with plant and production line setup from the relevant module.
The platform should log significant actions, failures, and user-visible events related to plant and production line setup for operational support.

FR-02.title: Inspection Plan Definition
requirements: The system must support inspection plan definition as a complete business workflow within the application.
Inspection Plan Definition should validate required inputs, enforce role-based permissions, and prevent invalid state changes.
Users must be able to review the current state and recent activity associated with inspection plan definition from the relevant module.
The platform should log significant actions, failures, and user-visible events related to inspection plan definition for operational support.

FR-03.title: Incoming Material Quality Checks
requirements: The system must support incoming material quality checks as a complete business workflow within the application.
Incoming Material Quality Checks should validate required inputs, enforce role-based permissions, and prevent invalid state changes.
Users must be able to review the current state and recent activity associated with incoming material quality checks from the relevant module.
The platform should log significant actions, failures, and user-visible events related to incoming material quality checks for operational support.

FR-04.title: In-process Inspection Recording
requirements: The system must support in-process inspection recording as a complete business workflow within the application.
In-process Inspection Recording should validate required inputs, enforce role-based permissions, and prevent invalid state changes.
Users must be able to review the current state and recent activity associated with in-process inspection recording from the relevant module.
The platform should log significant actions, failures, and user-visible events related to in-process inspection recording for operational support.

FR-05.title: Final Product Inspection Workflow
requirements: Authorized users must be able to create, update, and complete final product inspection workflow according to the approved business workflow.
The system should validate required fields, status transitions, and role-based actions before saving final product inspection workflow changes.
Final Product Inspection Workflow must maintain a clear history of assignments, approvals, or status updates for operational traceability.
The interface should surface the current workflow state, next available actions, and blocking conditions for final product inspection workflow.

FR-06.title: Defect Classification Catalog
requirements: The system must capture defect classification catalog data in real time or near real time as operational events occur.
Defect Classification Catalog should provide a timeline, status history, or change log so users can understand what happened and when.
Users must be able to review, filter, and reconcile defect classification catalog records against the related business entities.
The platform should flag missing, inconsistent, or exception conditions that require attention during defect classification catalog.

FR-07.title: Corrective Action Request Management
requirements: The system must provide end-to-end corrective action request management capabilities for creating, updating, viewing, and retiring relevant business records.
Corrective Action Request Management should support role-based permissions, validation rules, and duplicate-prevention checks where appropriate.
Users must be able to search, filter, and review historical activity related to corrective action request management without leaving the module context.
The platform should maintain auditability for important actions performed within corrective action request management and retain operational history for reporting.

FR-08.title: Non-conformance Case Tracking
requirements: The system must capture non-conformance case tracking data in real time or near real time as operational events occur.
Non-conformance Case Tracking should provide a timeline, status history, or change log so users can understand what happened and when.
Users must be able to review, filter, and reconcile non-conformance case tracking records against the related business entities.
The platform should flag missing, inconsistent, or exception conditions that require attention during non-conformance case tracking.

requirements: The system must support root cause analysis workspace as a complete business workflow within the application.
Root Cause Analysis Workspace should validate required inputs, enforce role-based permissions, and prevent invalid state changes.
Users must be able to review the current state and recent activity associated with root cause analysis workspace from the relevant module.
The platform should log significant actions, failures, and user-visible events related to root cause analysis workspace for operational support.

Users must be able to search, filter, and review historical activity related to supplier quality scorecards without leaving the module context.
The platform should maintain auditability for important actions performed within supplier quality scorecards and retain operational history for reporting.

requirements: The system must support regulatory compliance checklist as a complete business workflow within the application.

Users must be able to review the current state and recent activity associated with regulatory compliance checklist from the relevant module.

FR-18.title: Shift Quality Dashboard
requirements: The system must provide shift quality dashboard with role-appropriate filters, date ranges, and drill-down views.
Shift Quality Dashboard should calculate metrics from current transactional data and keep summaries aligned with source records.
Authorized users must be able to export or share shift quality dashboard outputs in formats used by business teams.
The platform should retain query history, report configuration, or dashboard preferences where that improves repeated analysis.

FR-19.title: Scrap and Yield Reporting
requirements: The system must provide scrap and yield reporting with role-appropriate filters, date ranges, and drill-down views.

FR-01.title: Design Plant and Production Line Setup UI
FR-01.description: Design the user interface for plant and production line setup. Create the screens, forms, dashboards, or status views needed by the SRS and make the workflow clear, responsive, and accessible for the target users.
acceptance_criteria: UI design for plant and production line setup is complete and reviewable.
The plant and production line setup design is responsive and accessible.
The design for plant and production line setup aligns with the documented user flow and validation states.

FR-01.title: Frontend Implementation of Plant and Production Line Setup
FR-01.description: Implement the frontend behavior for plant and production line setup. Build the required user interactions, validation states, loading states, and error handling so the interface supports these behaviors: The system must support plant and production line setup as a complete business workflow within the application; Plant and Production Line Setup should validate required inputs, enforce role-based permissions, and prevent invalid state changes; Users must be able to review the current state and recent activity associated with plant and production line setup from the relevant module.
acceptance_criteria: Frontend implements the required plant and production line setup user interactions.
Validation, loading, and error states for plant and production line setup are handled in the UI.
Frontend behavior for plant and production line setup is integrated correctly with backend APIs.

FR-01.title: Backend Implementation of Plant and Production Line Setup
FR-01.description: Implement the backend logic for plant and production line setup. Build the required APIs, business rules, validation, permissions, persistence, and processing needed to support these behaviors: The system must support plant and production line setup as a complete business workflow within the application; Plant and Production Line Setup should validate required inputs, enforce role-based permissions, and prevent invalid state changes; Users must be able to review the current state and recent activity associated with plant and production line setup from the relevant module.
acceptance_criteria: Backend logic for plant and production line setup is implemented and testable.
APIs and business rules for plant and production line setup enforce validation and security requirements.
Data handling for plant and production line setup is reliable and supports the required workflow.

FR-01.title: Security Hardening for Plant and Production Line Setup
FR-01.description: Implement the security controls required for plant and production line setup. Cover authentication, authorization, encryption, auditing, abuse prevention, and compliance checks where the SRS or workflow calls for them.
acceptance_criteria: Security controls for plant and production line setup enforce the required authentication, authorization, or data-protection rules.
Sensitive operations and failure scenarios for plant and production line setup are logged, validated, and protected against misuse.
The implementation of plant and production line setup satisfies the relevant privacy, audit, or compliance expectations in the SRS.

FR-01.title: Integrate Plant and Production Line Setup with External Services
FR-01.description: Integrate plant and production line setup with the external services or providers referenced by the SRS. Implement secure service connections, payload validation, error handling, and recovery behavior for any third-party dependency involved in the workflow.
acceptance_criteria: External or cross-system integrations required by plant and production line setup exchange the expected payloads successfully.
Retry, timeout, and failure handling for plant and production line setup integrations are implemented and observable.
Credentials, tokens, and outbound calls used by plant and production line setup are configured and secured correctly.

FR-01.title: Testing Plant and Production Line Setup Functionality
FR-01.description: Create and execute tests for plant and production line setup. Cover the main success flows, failure scenarios, permission checks, validation rules, integrations, and operational edge cases described in the SRS so the implementation is safe to release.
acceptance_criteria: Tests cover the main success and failure scenarios for plant and production line setup.
Security, validation, and permission checks for plant and production line setup are verified.
Regression coverage exists for the most important plant and production line setup behaviors.
